@graph.name: Agent Hand
@graph.description: Manage 5+ Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and AI agent sessions from one Rust TUI. Priority jump, fuzzy search, tmux isolation — free & open source.

offers.name: Max
offers.description: Monthly subscription — AI Session Summarizer, Remote Sharing, Session Relationships

@graph.featureList: tmux session management, AI agent status tracking, fuzzy search switching, priority jumping with Ctrl+N, groups and labels, dedicated tmux server isolation

acceptedAnswer.text: Agent Hand is a fast, Rust-based terminal session manager (TUI) for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. It uses tmux for isolation, letting you manage 5+ concurrent AI sessions from one dashboard with status detection, priority jump, and fuzzy search.

mainEntity.name: How do I install Agent Hand?

acceptedAnswer.text: Run: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weykon/agent-hand/master/install.sh | bash — works on macOS, Linux, and WSL. Or build from source with cargo build --release.

Yes, the core session manager is free and open source (MIT). There is an optional Pro tier ($19 one-time) for power features and a Max tier ($9/month) for AI summarizer, remote sharing, and session relationships.

mainEntity.name: Does Agent Hand work with Claude Code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Agent Hand was originally built to manage multiple Claude Code sessions. It detects session status (Waiting, Running, Idle), lets you priority-jump to sessions needing input with Ctrl+N, and provides fuzzy search across all sessions with Ctrl+G.

mainEntity.name: How is Agent Hand different from tmux?

acceptedAnswer.text: Agent Hand runs on top of tmux (using a dedicated server so it won't touch your config). It adds AI-agent-specific features: automatic status detection, priority jumping, session grouping, PTY monitoring, and a visual dashboard — things raw tmux doesn't provide.

@graph.name: How to Install Agent Hand
@graph.description: Install Agent Hand, a Rust-based terminal session manager for AI coding agents, on macOS, Linux, or WSL in under 30 seconds.

tool.name: Terminal (macOS Terminal, iTerm2, or any Linux terminal)

step.name: Run the install script
step.text: Open your terminal and run: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weykon/agent-hand/master/install.sh | bash

step.name: Launch Agent Hand
step.text: Run 'agent-hand' in your terminal. It will automatically create a dedicated tmux server and show the session dashboard.

step.name: Start managing AI sessions
step.text: Press 'n' to create a new session, launch your AI agent (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, etc.), and use Ctrl+N to priority-jump between sessions.

@graph.name: Agent Hand
@graph.description: Open-source developer tools for AI agent terminal session management. Built in Rust.
